entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: Pennisetum glaucum (L.) R. Br., the primary crop studied for population breeding in arid environments

entities.name: RAJASTHAN
entities.description: A desert region in India where pearl millet landraces are grown

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where field experiments were conducted

entities.name: DROUGHT STRESS
entities.description: Extreme water scarcity that affects the adaptation of modern cultivars

entities.name: ARID ENVIRONMENTS
entities.description: Dry environments characterized by low rainfall

entities.name: FAVORABLE GROWING CONDITIONS
entities.description: Environments that provide optimal resources for crop growth

entities.name: POPULATION BREEDING
entities.description: A breeding strategy used to exploit heterosis in pearl millet

entities.name: DIALLEL CROSSES
entities.description: A mating design used to evaluate heterotic patterns among breeding populations

entities.name: FIELD EXPERIMENTS
entities.description: Research trials conducted across eight environments in India

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced, denoted as GY

entities.name: STOVER YIELD
entities.description: The amount of vegetative biomass produced, denoted as SY

entities.name: TIME TO FLOWERING
entities.description: The duration from planting to flowering, denoted as TF

entities.name: TILLERING POTENTIAL
entities.description: The capacity of the plant to produce multiple tillers

entities.name: SEED SIZE
entities.description: The physical dimensions of the seeds produced

entities.name: HETEROSIS
entities.description: The superior performance of hybrid offspring compared to their parents

entities.name: MIDPARENT HETEROSIS
entities.description: Heterosis calculated as the deviation of the hybrid from the average of its parents

entities.name: GERMPLASM BASE
entities.description: The total genetic diversity available for breeding programs

entities.name: ADAPTATION
entities.description: The ability of a plant variety to survive and produce in a specific environment
